urdu transliteration text:
Beriyaan touk sambhaale, huwe bimaar chalaImam Sajjad is ill and wearing shackles around his neck and legsEnglish Transliteration
@HujjatELC

Beriyaan touk sambhaale, huwe bimaar chalaLeke beparda haram paabande baazaar chala
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
Imam Sajjad is ill and wearing shackles around his neck and legs,
He is leading the caravan of veil less women and children through the bazaar

urdu transliteration text:
Haae begoro kafan laash wo jalte khaimeBeridaayi wo sitamghaar wo sehme bache Khaak Abid pe yehi manzar-e-khunbaar chala
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
Oh the shroud less bodies, the burning tents,
the women without their veils, the oppressors, and the distraught children,
these are the scenes that Imam Sajjad witnessed on the desert of Karbala

urdu transliteration text:
Khaake ghash kis tarha baazaar mei bimaar giraTaaziyaane pei lipat karne sakina ne kahaa Is tarha durre naa Abid pe sitamghaar chala
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
When Imam Sajjad fell unconscious (due to his illness) in the bazaar,
Sakina held the whip and pleaded, “Do not strike my brother like this, you cruel man!”

urdu transliteration text:
touk-o-zanjeer ke zakhmon se lahu jaari haiPyaas hai dhoop ki shiddat hai ghashi paari hai Haae kis aalam-e-ghurbat mei who laachaar chala
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
The wounds of the chains & shackles have caused blood to flow,
There is extreme thirst, the scorching sun has caused unconsciousness,
In such a state of helplessness, has Imam Sajjad walked

urdu transliteration text:
Bhuj gaye zainabo kulsum ke sarse saayeAankh barsaai gayi aur kabhi pathar aaye Kaafela leke yatimon ka jo ghamkhwaar chala
@HujjatELC
english translation text:
The protectors of Zainab & Kulsoom are no longer,
Fire was thrown at them and sometimes stones,
This is how Imam Sajjad led the caravan of widows and orphans

Sometimes he looked at the head of Imam Husain and sometimes in the direction of Najaf,
As he walked towards the court of Yazid
